lifecycle_id_policy.notes: Scheme v2 (provisioned at 2026-07-03T00:00:00Z): value = offset + days_since_epoch * 4096 + blake2s-hash entropy (12 deterministic bits of kind+slug), base36, minimum width 5, no modulo — past 36^5 (~40 yr) IDs widen gracefully to 6 chars, never wrap. Lex/value order == time order for existing and new IDs (offset clears all pre-provisioning values); within a single day, IDs order by hash entropy, not mint time. node_bits is reserved (0 = full 12-bit hash entropy); hour_offset is v1-only and ignored under v2. Do not hand-edit epoch_utc, offset, or scheme_version after provisioning — issued IDs depend on them.

code_navigation_hints.notes: Project-tunable token lists referenced by seed-180/seed-211 edge-case pattern. Per wave 130rj-enh seeds-pattern-library-and-recipes: project conventions for guard / early-exit patterns vary even within a single language (Swift may use `guard`/`throw`/`assertionFailure`/`preconditionFailure`/`fatalError` plus project-internal helpers like `throwIfInvalid()`); hardcoding tokens in seeds would be wrong for many projects on day one. Each list is opt-in: leave empty to let the seed pattern describe the shape without concrete tokens.

setup.notes: Wave 1p9it: per-step deadlines (seconds) for each `wf setup` Phase-1 child, so a single stalled child fails loud with stage-specific guidance instead of hanging setup indefinitely. Keys: `venv_create_timeout_seconds` (default 300), `uv_bootstrap_timeout_seconds` (default 600), `dep_install_timeout_seconds` (default 1800), `model_warm_timeout_seconds` (default 1800), `index_build_stall_timeout_seconds` (default 1800). Every key is optional — the code defaults (sized for slow-but-legit environments: corp proxy, low-RAM WSL2) apply when a key is absent, non-numeric, or non-positive, so no numeric value is pre-seeded here; set a key only when a legitimately slow machine trips a default. `index_build_stall_timeout_seconds` is a NO-PROGRESS window (reset on each output line the index-build child emits), NOT a total build cap, so a long large-repo build that keeps emitting progress never trips it. Wave 1p9pe adds `hf_hub_download_timeout_seconds` (default 30) and `hf_hub_etag_timeout_seconds` (default 30): Hugging Face Hub SOCKET timeouts applied scoped around the model warm so a stalled fetch fails in-thread before the `model_warm_timeout_seconds` wall-clock deadline; they complement that deadline, never replace it. The download timeout is a per-chunk read timeout (a download that keeps making progress never trips it), and an operator-set `HF_HUB_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT`/`HF_HUB_ETAG_TIMEOUT` env var wins over these keys as the source of the value. Same optional/fail-safe semantics as the other keys.

docs_lint.notes: Docs-lint subprocess timeouts (seconds). `hook_timeout_seconds` (wave 1p9bg, default 120) bounds the incremental post-edit docs-lint hook (`--changed`). `full_scan_timeout_seconds` (wave 1p9iu, default 300) bounds the server-side FULL-corpus docs-lint scan run by the wave lifecycle tools (wave_validate / wave_prepare / wave_review / wave_close / wave_install_audit); on timeout those tools now report an actionable diagnostic naming this key instead of failing with a raw error. Both keys are optional — the code defaults apply when unset, so no numeric value is pre-seeded here. Raise `full_scan_timeout_seconds` on a large docs corpus whose full scan legitimately exceeds the default.

wave_review.non_waiver_rule: Wave Council may synthesize and escalate specialist findings, but it may not waive blocking required lanes by itself.

prepare.rotating_seat_policy: Select one domain seat from review triggers and wave evidence. Prefer docs-contract-reviewer for seed/prompt/behavioral-contract changes, performance-reviewer for indexing/search/hot-path changes, release-reviewer for packaging/distribution changes, or an applicable persona when operator-facing acceptance is central.

review.rotating_seat_policy: Re-evaluate the rotating seat from delivered behavior and completed specialist findings. Prefer the highest-salience domain lane or persona affected by what actually shipped.

design_system_policy.notes: Design-system surfaces may evolve within normal implementation scope. Set to 'read-only' to require explicit wave_gate_open(gate='design_system_edit_allowed') before any design-system change, or 'review-governed' to require reviewer-lane approval. When governance is 'evolvable', the design_system_edit_allowed gate is not enforced.
